 {"id":"caam-0eq","title":"Health status display in CLI","description":"# Health Status Display in CLI

## Purpose
Show profile health status indicators in CLI commands like `caam list` and `caam status`.

## User Experience Goals
Users should see at a glance:
- Which profiles are healthy
- Which profiles have issues
- What the issues are (expiring, errors, etc.)

## Display Formats

### `caam list` Output
```
$ caam list
Claude Profiles
  ● work@company.com      🟢 Pro    59m left   [ACTIVE]
    personal@gmail.com    🟡 Free   12m left   
    test@example.com      🔴 Expired            

Codex Profiles
  ● main                  🟢        23h left   [ACTIVE]
    backup                ⚪ Unknown            

Gemini Profiles
    default               🟢        Valid
```

### `caam status` Output
```
$ caam status
Active Profiles
───────────────────────────────────────────────────
claude    work@company.com    🟢 Healthy    59m left
codex     main                🟢 Healthy    23h left
gemini    (none)              

Warnings
───────────────────────────────────────────────────
claude/personal@gmail.com: Token expires in 12 minutes
claude/test@example.com: Token expired

Recommendations
───────────────────────────────────────────────────
• Run "caam refresh claude personal@gmail.com" to refresh expiring token
• Run "caam login claude test@example.com" to re-authenticate
```

### Status Column Format
```
🟢 59m left     - Healthy with time remaining
🟡 12m left     - Warning, expiring soon
🔴 Expired      - Critical, needs attention
🔴 3 errors     - Critical, multiple recent errors
⚪ Unknown      - Cannot determine status
```

## Technical Requirements

### Integration Points
```go
// In list command
profiles := store.List(provider)
for _, p := range profiles {
    health := healthStore.GetProfile(provider, p.Name)
    status, _ := health.CalculateHealth()
    fmt.Printf("  %s %s %s\n", 
        activeIndicator(p),
        p.Name,
        formatHealthStatus(status, health))
}
```

### Helper Functions
```go
func formatHealthStatus(status HealthStatus, h *ProfileHealth) string
func formatTimeRemaining(expiry time.Time) string
func formatStatusWithReason(status HealthStatus, h *ProfileHealth) string
```

## Color Support
Use lipgloss styles consistent with TUI:
- 🟢 Green for healthy
- 🟡 Yellow for warning
- 🔴 Red for critical
- Gray for unknown

Support `--no-color` flag for piping.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `caam list` shows health status for each profile
- [ ] `caam status` shows active profiles with health
- [ ] `caam status` shows warnings and recommendations
- [ ] Time remaining formatted naturally (59m, 23h, 3d)
- [ ] Colors disabled with --no-color or non-TTY
- [ ] Unit tests for formatting functions

## Files to Create/Modify
- `cmd/caam/cmd/list.go` (modify)
- `cmd/caam/cmd/status.go` (modify)
- `internal/health/format.go` (new)
- `internal/health/format_test.go` (new)

## Dependencies
- Health status calculation (caam-3ef)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:18:49.689326323-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T17:26:54.046325238-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T17:26:54.046325238-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented health status display in CLI.  {"id":"caam-0gb","title":"Smart Profile Management configuration","description":"## Purpose
Create unified configuration for Smart Profile Management features.

## Background
Multiple features need configuration:
- Refresh threshold (default 10 minutes)
- Health status thresholds
- Penalty decay rate
- Data retention days
- File watching enabled/disabled

Rather than scatter these settings, create a unified config system.

## Configuration Location
~/.caam/config.yaml

## Schema
```yaml
# ~/.caam/config.yaml
version: 1

# Health & Refresh settings
health:
  refresh_threshold: 10m        # Refresh tokens expiring within this time
  warning_threshold: 1h         # Yellow status below this TTL
  penalty_decay_rate: 0.8       # 20% decay every 5 minutes
  penalty_decay_interval: 5m
  
# Analytics settings
analytics:
  enabled: true
  retention_days: 90            # Keep detailed logs
  aggregate_retention_days: 365
  cleanup_on_startup: true
  
# Runtime settings
runtime:
  file_watching: true
  reload_on_sighup: true
  pid_file: true

# Project associations
project:
  enabled: true
  auto_activate: false          # Auto-activate based on CWD
```

## Technical Implementation

### Config Package
```go
// internal/config/config.go
type Config struct {
    Version   int            `yaml:"version"`
    Health    HealthConfig   `yaml:"health"`
    Analytics AnalyticsConfig `yaml:"analytics"`
    Runtime   RuntimeConfig  `yaml:"runtime"`
    Project   ProjectConfig  `yaml:"project"`
}

type HealthConfig struct {
    RefreshThreshold     time.Duration `yaml:"refresh_threshold"`
    WarningThreshold     time.Duration `yaml:"warning_threshold"`
    PenaltyDecayRate     float64       `yaml:"penalty_decay_rate"`
    PenaltyDecayInterval time.Duration `yaml:"penalty_decay_interval"`
}

// DefaultConfig returns sensible defaults
func DefaultConfig() *Config

// Load reads config from file, falling back to defaults
func Load() (*Config, error)

// Save writes config to file
func (c *Config) Save() error
```

### CLI Integration
```bash
# View current config
caam config show

# Set a value
caam config set health.refresh_threshold 5m

# Reset to defaults
caam config reset
```

## Files to Create
- internal/config/config.go
- internal/config/config_test.go
- cmd/caam/config.go

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Config file parsed from ~/.caam/config.yaml
- [ ] Sensible defaults when file missing
- [ ] CLI commands for viewing and editing
- [ ] Duration parsing works (10m, 1h, etc.)
- [ ] Unknown fields ignored (forward compatible)
- [ ] Validation of values (no negative durations)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:31:42.196043884-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T17:13:15.110015636-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T17:13:15.110015636-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented SPM configuration with YAML support, CLI commands, and tests"}
 {"id":"caam-0gx","title":"File system watcher for profile changes","description":"## Purpose
Implement file system watching to detect profile changes in real-time using fsnotify.

## Background
codex-pool reloads credentials without restart:
```go
POST /admin/reload → h.pool.replace(newAccounts)
```

For caam, we watch the profiles directory and notify when files change.
This enables the TUI to auto-refresh without manual intervention.

## Watched Paths
```
~/.caam/profiles/        # Profile storage directory
~/.caam/profiles/claude/ # Provider subdirectories
~/.caam/profiles/codex/
~/.caam/profiles/gemini/
```

## Technical Implementation
```go
// internal/watcher/watcher.go
package watcher

import (
    "github.com/fsnotify/fsnotify"
)

// Event types
type EventType int

const (
    EventProfileAdded EventType = iota
    EventProfileModified
    EventProfileDeleted
)

// Event represents a file system change
type Event struct {
    Type     EventType
    Provider string
    Profile  string
    Path     string
}

// Watcher monitors profile directories for changes
type Watcher struct {
    fsWatcher *fsnotify.Watcher
    events    chan Event
    errors    chan error
    done      chan struct{}
}

// New creates a new profile watcher
func New(profilesDir string) (*Watcher, error) {
    fsw, err := fsnotify.NewWatcher()
    if err != nil {
        return nil, err
    }
    
    w := &Watcher{
        fsWatcher: fsw,
        events:    make(chan Event, 100),
        errors:    make(chan error, 10),
        done:      make(chan struct{}),
    }
    
    // Watch base directory and all provider subdirs
    if err := fsw.Add(profilesDir); err != nil {
        return nil, err
    }
    
    // Watch each provider directory
    for _, provider := range []string{"claude", "codex", "gemini"} {
        providerDir := filepath.Join(profilesDir, provider)
        if _, err := os.Stat(providerDir); err == nil {
            fsw.Add(providerDir)
        }
    }
    
    go w.run()
    return w, nil
}

func (w *Watcher) run() {
    for {
        select {
        case event := <-w.fsWatcher.Events:
            if e := w.translateEvent(event); e != nil {
                w.events <- *e
            }
        case err := <-w.fsWatcher.Errors:
            w.errors <- err
        case <-w.done:
            return
        }
    }
}

func (w *Watcher) translateEvent(e fsnotify.Event) *Event {
    // Parse path to extract provider and profile name
    // Filter out non-profile files (e.g., .DS_Store)
    // Debounce rapid successive events
}

func (w *Watcher) Events() <-chan Event { return w.events }
func (w *Watcher) Errors() <-chan error { return w.errors }
func (w *Watcher) Close() error { close(w.done); return w.fsWatcher.Close() }
```

## Event Debouncing
File saves often trigger multiple events. Debounce to avoid redundant refreshes:
```go
type debouncer struct {
    pending map[string]time.Time
    delay   time.Duration
}

func (d *debouncer) shouldEmit(path string) bool {
    if last, ok := d.pending[path]; ok {
        if time.Since(last) < d.delay {
            return false
        }
    }
    d.pending[path] = time.Now()
    return true
}
```

## Files to Create
- internal/watcher/watcher.go
- internal/watcher/watcher_test.go
- internal/watcher/debounce.go

## Dependencies
- github.com/fsnotify/fsnotify

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Detects profile additions, modifications, deletions
- [ ] Correctly identifies provider and profile from path
- [ ] Debounces rapid events (configurable delay, default 100ms)
- [ ] Handles directory creation (new provider added)
- [ ] Clean shutdown without goroutine leaks
- [ ] Ignores non-profile files (.DS_Store, .git, etc.)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:24:02.784501695-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T19:25:37.771554993-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T19:25:37.771554993-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented fsnotify-based profile watcher with debounced events, recursive dir watching (no symlinks), provider/profile parsing, and tests.","comments":[{"id":11,"issue_id":"caam-0gx","author":"ubuntu","text":"Starting implementation of internal/watcher using fsnotify to emit debounced profile add/modify/delete events for ~/.local/share/caam/profiles/<provider>/<name>/... . Will include translate logic + unit tests.","created_at":"2025-12-18T00:11:00Z"},{"id":12,"issue_id":"caam-0gx","author":"ubuntu","text":"Implemented internal/watcher using fsnotify: recursive directory watching (no symlinks), profile-level event translation (provider/profile from path), debounced modified events (default 100ms), provider-dir bootstrap scan so first-run provider+profile creation emits ProfileAdded reliably. Added fsnotify dependency + unit/integration tests. (Agent mail not configured in this env; using issue comments for coordination.)","created_at":"2025-12-18T00:25:24Z"}]}
+{"id":"caam-0gy","title":"Session resume support for Codex profiles","description":"# Session Resume Support for Codex Profiles

## The Killer Use Case: Usage Limit → Switch Account → Resume Session

When you hit usage limits, Codex outputs:
\`\`\`
■ You've hit your usage limit. Visit https://chatgpt.com/codex/settings/usage...
Token usage: total=1,605,319 input=1,346,660 (+ 69,804,160 cached) output=258,659
To continue this session, run codex resume 019b2e3d-b524-7c22-91da-47de9068d09a
\`\`\`

**This is caam's superpower moment:**
\`\`\`bash
# Hit limit on current account
# Codex shows: "To continue this session, run codex resume 019b2e3d-..."

# Instant account switch (< 1 second!)
caam activate codex backup-account

# Resume session with NEW account
codex resume 019b2e3d-b524-7c22-91da-47de9068d09a "proceed"
\`\`\`

The session context is preserved, but you're now using a fresh account with more tokens!

## Commands

### caam resume - Dedicated resume command
\`\`\`bash
# Resume most recent session
caam resume codex work

# Resume specific session (from Codex's output)
caam resume codex work --session 019b2e3d-b524-7c22-91da-47de9068d09a

# Resume with prompt to continue
caam resume codex work "proceed"
caam resume codex work --session <uuid> "proceed"
\`\`\`

### Enhanced caam exec - Auto-capture session
\`\`\`bash
# Normal exec now captures session ID for later resume
caam exec codex work
# On exit, stores last session ID in profile metadata

# Later, resume with:
caam resume codex work
\`\`\`

## Implementation

### 1. Session ID capture in exec
Parse Codex output for session ID patterns and store in profile:
- On normal exit: capture session from output
- On usage limit: capture from "To continue this session, run codex resume <uuid>"

### 2. Profile metadata storage
\`\`\`yaml
# ~/.local/share/caam/profiles/codex/work/profile.yaml
last_session_id: "019b2e3d-b524-7c22-91da-47de9068d09a"
last_session_ts: "2025-12-17T20:11:00Z"
\`\`\`

### 3. Resume command
\`\`\`go
func runResume(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
    tool := args[0]
    profileName := args[1]
    
    // Get session ID from flag or profile metadata
    sessionID := sessionFlag
    if sessionID == "" {
        sessionID = prof.LastSessionID
    }
    if sessionID == "" {
        return fmt.Errorf("no session to resume. Start a session first with: caam exec %s %s", tool, profileName)
    }
    
    // Build codex resume command
    codexArgs := []string{"resume", sessionID}
    if len(args) > 2 {
        codexArgs = append(codexArgs, args[2]) // prompt like "proceed"
    }
    
    return execWithProfile(ctx, prof, "codex", codexArgs...)
}
\`\`\`

## Workflow: Headless Server with Usage Limits

\`\`\`bash
# Day 1: Setup on headless server
ssh devserver
caam login codex primary --device-code    # Auth primary account
caam login codex backup --device-code     # Auth backup account

# Start working
caam exec codex primary
# ... work until usage limit hit ...
# Codex: "To continue this session, run codex resume 019b2e3d-..."

# Instant switch + resume!
caam activate codex backup
caam resume codex backup --session 019b2e3d-... "proceed"
# Continues exactly where you left off, new account!

# Later, switch back when primary resets
caam activate codex primary
\`\`\`

## Device Auth Flow

Codex device auth looks like:
\`\`\`
Follow these steps to sign in with ChatGPT using device code authorization:

1. Open this link in your browser and sign in to your account
   https://auth.openai.com/codex/device

2. Enter this one-time code (expires in 15 minutes)
   FXZV-2QEFS

Device codes are a common phishing target. Never share this code.
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] \`caam resume codex <profile>\` resumes last session
- [ ] \`caam resume codex <profile> --session <uuid>\` resumes specific session
- [ ] \`caam resume codex <profile> "proceed"\` resumes with prompt
- [ ] \`caam exec\` captures session ID for later resume
- [ ] Cross-profile resume works (switch account, resume session)

## Why This Matters

This is **caam's unique value**: no other tool lets you:
1. Hit a usage limit
2. Switch accounts in < 1 second
3. Resume your exact session with the new account

This turns usage limits from a workflow-killer into a minor inconvenience.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T20:12:30.022248297-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T20:13:17.286983963-05:00"}

 {"id":"caam-nac","title":"Implement browser launcher abstraction","description":"## Task
Create internal/browser/browser.go with a Launcher interface and implementations for different browsers.

## Design
```go
type Launcher interface {
    // Open opens a URL in the configured browser profile
    Open(url string) error
    // Name returns the browser name for display
    Name() string
}

type Config struct {
    Command    string // Browser executable
    ProfileDir string // Profile directory or name
}
```

## Implementations Needed
1. ChromeLauncher - uses --profile-directory flag
2. FirefoxLauncher - uses -P flag for profile name
3. DefaultLauncher - uses OS default (open/xdg-open)

## Platform Considerations
- macOS: Chrome is at /Applications/Google Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google Chrome
- Linux: Usually 'google-chrome' or 'chromium-browser'
- Windows: Complex paths, need registry lookup or common paths

## Rationale
Abstraction allows us to support multiple browsers and add new ones easily. The interface also makes testing straightforward.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Launcher interface defined
- Chrome implementation works on macOS and Linux
- Firefox implementation works
- Default fallback works
- Unit tests for each implementation","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:50:26.47883372-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T01:16:04.783685539-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T01:16:04.783685539-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented browser launcher abstraction with Launcher interface, ChromeLauncher (--profile-directory), FirefoxLauncher (-P), DefaultLauncher (xdg-open/open), and GenericLauncher. Includes platform-specific browser detection for macOS/Linux/Windows.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-nac","depends_on_id":"caam-4eg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:50:26.499689437-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"caam-pb7","title":"Enhance lock file with PID validation","description":"## Task
Improve the lock file system to detect stale locks from dead processes.

## Current Lock Format
```json
{"pid": 12345, "locked_at": "2024-01-15T10:00:00Z"}
```

## Enhanced Implementation
1. Read lock file
2. Check if PID still exists (os.FindProcess + signal 0)
3. If process dead, consider lock stale
4. Optionally auto-clean stale locks

## Platform Considerations
- Unix: kill(pid, 0) returns error if process doesn't exist
- Windows: OpenProcess + different error handling

## Code Location
- internal/profile/profile.go - IsLocked(), Lock(), Unlock()
- Add: IsLockStale(), CleanStaleLock()

## Acceptance Criteria
- Can detect if locking process is still running
- Stale locks reported appropriately
- Works on Linux and macOS","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:53:08.139140246-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T01:24:04.458474061-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T01:24:04.458474061-05:00","close_reason":"Added LockInfo struct, GetLockInfo(), IsProcessAlive(), IsLockStale(), CleanStaleLock(), and LockWithCleanup() methods. The exec runner now uses LockWithCleanup to automatically clean stale locks from dead processes.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-pb7","depends_on_id":"caam-pxg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:53:29.6771176-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"caam-peb","title":"Usage analytics TUI panel","description":"## Purpose
Add a usage statistics panel to the TUI showing profile usage patterns visually.

## Background
The TUI provides a richer interface than CLI. Usage stats can be displayed in a
dedicated panel or overlay, accessible via keystroke.

## Design Mockup
```
┌─ Usage Statistics ─────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                                                             │
│  Last 7 days                                               │
│                                                             │
│  claude/work         ████████████████████  42 sess  18.5h  │
│  codex/main          ████████████████      28 sess  12.1h  │
│  claude/personal     ██████                12 sess   4.2h  │
│  gemini/default      █                      3 sess   0.5h  │
│                                                             │
│  Total: 85 sessions, 35.3 hours                            │
│                                                             │
│  Press [u] to toggle, [1-4] for time range                 │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Keyboard Shortcuts
- `u` - Toggle usage panel visibility
- `1` - Last 24 hours
- `2` - Last 7 days (default)
- `3` - Last 30 days
- `4` - All time
- `esc` - Close panel

## Technical Implementation
```go
// internal/tui/usage_panel.go
type UsagePanel struct {
    visible   bool
    timeRange int // 1=24h, 7=week, 30=month, 0=all
    stats     []ProfileUsage
    width     int
    height    int
    styles    UsagePanelStyles
}

type ProfileUsage struct {
    Provider     string
    ProfileName  string
    SessionCount int
    TotalHours   float64
    Percentage   float64 // For bar chart
}

func (u *UsagePanel) SetStats(stats []ProfileUsage)
func (u *UsagePanel) Toggle()
func (u *UsagePanel) SetTimeRange(days int)
func (u *UsagePanel) View() string

// Bar chart rendering
func renderBar(percentage float64, width int) string {
    filled := int(percentage * float64(width))
    return strings.Repeat("█", filled) + strings.Repeat(" ", width-filled)
}
```

### Integration with Main Model
```go
// internal/tui/model.go
type Model struct {
    // ... existing fields
    usagePanel *UsagePanel
}

func (m Model) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
    switch msg := msg.(type) {
    case tea.KeyMsg:
        if key.Matches(msg, m.keys.Usage) {
            m.usagePanel.Toggle()
            if m.usagePanel.visible {
                return m, m.loadUsageStats()
            }
        }
    }
}
```

## Files to Create/Modify
- internal/tui/usage_panel.go
- internal/tui/usage_panel_test.go
- internal/tui/model.go (add usagePanel field)
- internal/tui/keys.go (add Usage keybinding)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Panel toggles with u key
- [ ] Bar chart shows relative usage
- [ ] Time range switching works
- [ ] Stats load asynchronously (no UI freeze)
- [ ] Graceful handling when no data available
- [ ] Panel respects terminal size","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:23:16.833524781-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T16:23:16.833524781-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-peb","depends_on_id":"caam-r36","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:29:02.150717061-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"caam-peb","depends_on_id":"caam-cfb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:29:07.256718993-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"caam-pxg","title":"Session Management","description":"## Overview
Enhance the existing lock file system to provide robust session tracking and prevent data corruption when multiple processes try to use the same profile.

## Current State
- Basic lock files exist (profile.Lock(), profile.Unlock())
- Lock files contain simple JSON with PID and timestamp

## Problems to Solve
1. Stale locks: If a process crashes, the lock remains
2. No visibility: Can't see what sessions are running
3. No protection: Operations don't always check locks

## Goals
- Detect and clean up stale locks (process no longer running)
- Show running sessions in CLI and TUI
- Prevent destructive operations on locked profiles
- Allow force-unlock for stuck locks

## User Value
- Prevents accidental data corruption
- Makes it safe to run multiple caam instances
- Visibility into what's running","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:53:03.044026907-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T01:42:02.293200799-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T01:42:02.293200799-05:00","close_reason":"All child tasks completed: sessions list, lock file PID validation, and force-unlock commands."}
 {"id":"caam-pyd","title":"Health status display in TUI","description":"# Health Status Display in TUI

## Purpose
Show profile health status indicators in the TUI profiles panel.

## Design

### Profile Row Layout
```
┌─ Claude Profiles ────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Name              Auth   Status        Last Used   Account   │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ ● work@company    oauth  🟢 59m left   2h ago      Acme Inc  │
│   personal@gmail  oauth  🟡 12m left   1d ago      -         │
│   test@example    oauth  🔴 Expired    3d ago      -         │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Status Column
Replace current simple status with health-aware status:
- Current: ✓ (logged in) or ✗ (not logged in)
- New: 🟢🟡🔴⚪ with time/reason

### Column Width Adjustment
```go
colWidths := struct {
    name     int
    auth     int
    status   int  // Increase for "🟢 59m left"
    lastUsed int
    account  int
}{
    name:     16,
    auth:     8,
    status:   14,  // Was 10, now needs more for time
    lastUsed: 12,
    account:  16,
}
```

### Status Bar Enhancement
Show aggregated health in the status bar:
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ 3 profiles: 2 healthy, 1 warning │ Press ? for help        │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Technical Requirements

### ProfileInfo Enhancement
```go
type ProfileInfo struct {
    Name      string
    AuthMode  string
    LoggedIn  bool
    Locked    bool
    LastUsed  time.Time
    Account   string
    IsActive  bool
    // New fields:
    HealthStatus  HealthStatus
    TokenExpiry   time.Time
    ErrorCount    int
    Penalty       float64
}
```

### Styles
```go
func (s ProfilesPanelStyles) StatusStyle(status HealthStatus) lipgloss.Style {
    switch status {
    case StatusHealthy:
        return s.StatusOK  // green
    case StatusWarning:
        return lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(colorYellow)
    case StatusCritical:
        return s.StatusBad  // red
    default:
        return lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(colorGray)
    }
}
```

### Status Formatting
```go
func formatTUIStatus(pi *ProfileInfo) string {
    icon := pi.HealthStatus.Icon()
    
    if pi.TokenExpiry.IsZero() {
        return icon + " Unknown"
    }
    
    ttl := time.Until(pi.TokenExpiry)
    if ttl <= 0 {
        return icon + " Expired"
    }
    
    return icon + " " + formatDuration(ttl)
}
```

## Detail Panel Enhancement
Show full health details when profile is selected:
```
┌─ Profile Details ────────────────────────┐
│ work@company.com                         │
│                                          │
│ Status:    🟢 Healthy                    │
│ Token:     Expires in 59 minutes         │
│ Plan:      Pro                           │
│ Errors:    0 in last hour                │
│ Penalty:   0.0                           │
│                                          │
│ [Enter] Activate  [l] Refresh  [d] Delete│
└──────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Health status icons in profile list
- [ ] Time remaining displayed for each profile
- [ ] Status bar shows health summary
- [ ] Detail panel shows full health info
- [ ] Colors match health status
- [ ] Updates when health data changes

## Files to Create/Modify
- `internal/tui/profiles_panel.go` (modify)
- `internal/tui/detail_panel.go` (modify)
- `internal/tui/model.go` (modify)
- `internal/tui/styles.go` (modify)

## Dependencies
- Health status calculation (caam-3ef)
- Health metadata storage (caam-6gj)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:18:56.315192859-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T17:24:57.8927832-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T17:24:57.8927832-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented health status display in TUI profiles panel and detail panel. Updated models and styles to support health visualization.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-pyd","depends_on_id":"caam-3ef","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:27:42.624440315-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"caam-qa1","title":"Add --browser flag to profile add command","description":"## Task
Extend 'caam profile add' to accept browser configuration during profile creation.

## New Flags
- --browser: Browser type (chrome, firefox, default)
- --browser-profile: Browser profile name or directory

## Example Usage
```bash
caam profile add codex work --browser chrome --browser-profile 'Profile 2'
caam profile add claude personal --browser firefox --browser-profile 'personal-firefox'
```

## Implementation
- Add flags to profileAddCmd in cmd/caam/cmd/root.go
- Pass values to profile.Create()
- Store in profile.json

## UX Consideration
Could also support interactive browser selection if flags not provided. List detected Chrome profiles and let user choose.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Flags accepted and stored in profile
- Profile loads correctly with browser config
- Works with existing profiles (backwards compatible)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:50:51.770335947-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T01:21:48.713708067-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T01:21:48.713708067-05:00","close_reason":"Added --browser, --browser-profile, and --browser-name flags to 'profile add' command. Profile status now shows browser config.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-qa1","depends_on_id":"caam-4eg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:50:51.784339072-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"caam-qa1","depends_on_id":"caam-ypx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:50:51.785523973-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
